nav.tab_disabled_home: Open a project card from the dashboard first

header.upgrade_title: Upgrade your plan — more usage and features
header.manage_sub_title: Manage subscription — payment method, invoices, cancel
header.team_title: Teams — create a team and invite members
header.stop_jobs_title: Stop background jobs — frees the screen immediately
header.stopped_toast: Background jobs stopped. Already-processed results will appear after a refresh.

usage.title_exhausted: You've reached your limit — click to view details in your profile

ai_draft.title: This draft needs your review.
ai_draft.body_with_label: Skim through the {label} to check it's correct, and edit it yourself if needed. We recommend a human reviews important decisions.
ai_draft.body: Skim through it to check it's correct, and edit it yourself if needed. We recommend a human reviews important decisions.

project_not_ready.title_no_meeting: Please write a meeting log first
project_not_ready.title_processing: AI is generating your CPS · PRD
project_not_ready.desc_no_meeting_html:
{feature} is available only after Meeting Log → CPS → PRD are all in place.
Write your first meeting log on the Plan page and AI will automatically carry on with the next steps.
project_not_ready.desc_processing_html:
Once you save a meeting log on Plan, CPS · PRD are generated in the background, which takes about 2–6 minutes.
{feature} will open automatically when it's done — please check back shortly.

project_lookup.new_title: Create '{name}' as new (registered as OWNS on next mutation)
project_lookup.selected_new: '{name}' selected. Upload a meeting log on the Plan page to officially register it.

mcp.intro_html: This hands your coding AI (Cursor · Claude Code) a "spec & design map" of this project. Once connected, when you open a code file the AI looks up which spec it came from and which screens/features it ties into — on its own. → Fewer off-base code suggestions.
mcp.how_step1_html: Click [Issue new MCP token] below to create a key (token). — shown only once
mcp.how_step2_html: Copy the config that appears with it → paste into your IDE. — Cursor box for Cursor, Claude box for Claude Code
mcp.how_step3_html: Restart your IDE → done! The AI now reads this project.
mcp.glossary_html: MCP — a 'pipe' connecting AI tools to your project. · Token — a secret access key (don't share). · IDE — your code editor (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.).
mcp.access_token_hint_html: — Shown only once after issuing. The issue dialog provides a complete snippet with the token included.
mcp.config_format_hint_html: — The issue dialog has a complete version with the token embedded. This is just a format preview. When using .mcp.json at the project root, you must add it to .gitignore.
mcp.tool_find_spec: file path → the Story / Aggregate / API / Service this file implements
mcp.tool_trace_upstream: Design node → PRD Story → Epic chain
mcp.tool_list_design_nodes: all design nodes in the project + Story summaries
mcp.tool_get_story: Story details + derived design nodes + screens
mcp.tool_search_spec: full-text search across spec items (name + description)
mcp.tool_search_skills: keyword search across project AI coding rules (Skills)
mcp.tool_get_prd: fetch the project's master PRD (full text)
mcp.tool_get_cps: fetch the project's master CPS (core problem summary)
mcp.tool_get_api_spec: fetch API contracts (method, endpoint, params, body, error, auth)
mcp.tool_get_screen_spec: fetch screen definitions + called APIs and screen transitions
mcp.tool_get_lint_findings: fetch design items missing from code in the last lint run
mcp.footnote: MCP (Model Context Protocol) — Anthropic standard. Supported by Cursor 0.45+ and Claude Code.
mcp.issue_label_intro: Enter a label to identify where this token will be used. It cannot be changed after issuing.
mcp.issued_warning_html: This token is shown only once, right now. Save it somewhere safe before confirming.
mcp.token_standalone_label: Token (standalone — for entering directly into other tools)
mcp.full_config_label_html: Complete IDE config (Cursor ~/.cursor/mcp.json · Claude Code project root .mcp.json)
mcp.expires_note_html: Expires: {date} (90 days) · When using .mcp.json at the project root, be sure to add .mcp.json to .gitignore.
mcp.save_confirm: I have saved it somewhere safe and understand I won't be able to see it again once this window is closed.
mcp.list_empty: You haven't issued any MCP tokens yet.
mcp.revoke_message:
Revoking the "{label}" token.
IDEs currently using it will immediately get 401.
Continue?

upgrade.mailto_body:
Hello,

I'd like to request an upgrade to the Harness {tier} ({price}) tier.
- Email: (your sign-up email)
- Current tier: {current}
- Reason: 

Thank you.
upgrade.contact_body:
Hello,

I've reached the Harness Pro Max limit and would like to inquire about additional capacity.
- Email: (your sign-up email)
- Reason: 

Thank you.
upgrade.subtitle_exceeded_generic: You've used up your current tier's usage limit.
upgrade.subtitle_default: Need more meeting registrations and AI analysis?
upgrade.selffix_html: ✂️ If you trim your meeting log to {limit} characters or fewer, you can save it right away on your current tier. Upgrading lets you process longer meeting logs in one go.
upgrade.reset_notice_days_html: 💡 In {days} days your usage will auto-reset and your limit will recover. If you want to use it sooner, upgrade to a tier below.
upgrade.reset_notice_today: 💡 Reset scheduled for today — your usage will reset shortly.
upgrade.note: Your subscription begins immediately after registering a card. When upgrading, only the prorated difference for remaining days is charged.

my_projects.title: My projects
my_projects.github_start_title: Start from a GitHub repo URL (AI auto-generates V1)
my_projects.empty_desc: Create your first project with the "New project" button.
my_projects.load_failed: Failed to load the project list.
my_projects.select_new_toast: '{name}' selected. It will be officially registered when you upload a meeting log on the Plan page.
my_projects.create_failed: Failed to create the project. Please try again.
my_projects.name_invalid_char: '::' cannot be used in the project name.
my_projects.delete_confirm_message:
All data for the '{name}' project (meeting logs, CPS, PRD, Design, Lint, etc.) will be permanently deleted.

This action cannot be undone.

web_vitals.title: Page performance history
web_vitals.intro_html: Five speed metrics measured automatically every time a page loads. Classified as 🟢 Good / 🟡 Needs improvement / 🔴 Poor.
web_vitals.empty: No measurements yet. They accumulate automatically as you use the pages.
web_vitals.help_summary: How do I read this table?
web_vitals.help_recent_html: Recent — the single most recent measurement. How this page just performed.
web_vitals.help_typical_html: Typical (50%) — the median measurement — the speed an average user experiences.
web_vitals.help_p75_html: 75% — 3 out of 4 users are at least this fast. The value Google uses to rate sites.
web_vitals.help_p95_html: 95% — everyone except the slowest 5% is this fast. A worst-case check.
web_vitals.help_good_html: Good threshold — Google's cutoff for a "fast page". Differs per metric.
web_vitals.help_status_html: Status — auto-classified based on the recent value. 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴.
web_vitals.help_note_html: {count} records total. The more often you visit the same page, the more accurate the distribution. The Core Web Vitals (affecting Google search ranking) are LCP · CLS · INP.
web_vitals.clear_confirm_message: Delete all saved Web Vitals measurement history?

status.no_project: Pick a project or start a new one first.
status.plan_need_meeting: Start by jotting down a meeting log or your raw idea.
status.plan_need_cps: Time to create the core summary (CPS).
status.plan_done: Planning is done. Design is next.
status.design_locked: Finish the spec (PRD) first to start designing.
status.design_todo: Now design the screens, features, and structure.
status.design_progress: Design in progress — fill in the rest.
status.design_done: Design is complete. Next is connecting code.
status.code_need_repo: Connect a GitHub repo to browse the code.
status.code_done: Repo connected. Move on to the code review.
status.lint_ready: Run the code review. When it's done you can export the deliverables.
status.deliver_ready: Ready to export. Pick the deliverables you need.

library.remove_confirm_message: This removes '{label}' from your library. The repository and its analysis results are not deleted.
library.remove_failed: Couldn't remove it. Please try again in a moment.
